market_context: US stock futures are strongly higher in pre-market, with S&P 500 futures at 6,676.75 (+1.80%), Dow Jones futures at 46,760.00 (+1.89%), and Nasdaq 100 futures at 24,557.75 (+1.89%), rebounding from Friday's plunge amid US plans to escalate Iran war[2][3]. Oil prices tumbled sharply with WTI at 90.11 (-8.27%) on U.S.-Iran talks, boosting sentiment as stock index futures rally[2][3]. Robinhood prediction markets show optimism for S&P 500 Jun-26 futures above 6475 at 77¢ probability[1].

predictions.reasoning: S&P 500 futures up 1.80% to 6,676.75 pre-market after Friday's -1.51% drop, signaling strong rebound open[2][3]. Robinhood markets price 77% chance Jun-26 futures above 6475, with 68% for above 6600[1]. Oil plunge on U.S.-Iran talks supports risk-on sentiment[3].

Nasdaq 100 futures +1.89% to 24,557.75, mirroring S&P strength post-Friday's -1.88% decline[2][3]. Tech-heavy index benefits from oil tumble (-8.27% WTI) easing energy costs[2]. Prediction markets align with bullish S&P outlook[1].

Dow Jones futures +1.89% to 46,760 after -0.96% Friday close, driven by futures rally on falling oil[2][3]. Geopolitical de-escalation via U.S.-Iran talks counters prior war escalation fears[3]. Broad market pre-market gains support industrials[2].

WTI oil futures -8.27% to 90.11 on U.S.-Iran talks, extending commodity selloff[2][3]. Brent also -7.48% to 101.36, pressuring energy ETFs like USO[2]. Sharp pre-market drop likely persists amid risk-on shift[3].
